name: Lisas Penuche Fudge
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 9x5x3" loaf pan with foil. Butter the pan and set aside.
2. Butter the sides of a heavy saucepan.
3. Combine sugar, brown sugar, cream, and milk in the pan and cook over medium-high heat to boiling. Stir constantly with a wooden spoon. Cook until sugar dissolves, about 5 minutes.
4. Clip on a candy thermometer. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring frequently, until the thermometer reaches 236°F (soft ball stage). The mixture should boil at a steady rate over the entire surface. Reaching soft ball should take 15-20 minutes.
5. Remove the pan from the heat. Add the butter and vanilla. Do not stir.
6. Cool without stirring to 110°F. This should take about 50 minutes.
7. Remove the thermometer from the pan and beat the fudge with a wooden spoon until just thickening. Add nuts. Continue beating until the fudge gets very thick and begins to lose its gloss, this should take about 10 minutes total.
8. Pour into the prepared pan. When the candy is firm, use the foil liner to lift from the pan and cut into squares. Store covered. Makes 1 1/4 pounds.
